verdict.answer_first: A states the actionable choice immediately: "when my nodes lose contact, do I want the system to refuse writes (stay correct) or keep accepting them (stay up)?"
verdict.decisiveness: B gives a prescriptive, numbered list: "Practical decision recipe... 2. Default to CP/strong consistency unless you have a scale or latency reason not to"
verdict.actionability: B's "Practical decision recipe" is a checklist I can execute right now, making it more actionable than A's more general framework.
